articles.title: WHY IS THERE ALWAYS A MEETING BOT IN YOUR ZOOM CALL?
→ Get started with $100 in free credits
BUILDING BULLETPROOF REACT COMPONENTS
articles.summary: “Bulletproof" React components are designed to withstand the "hostile" real-world conditions beyond the typical happy path. Fragile components often break when encountering challenges like server rendering, hydration, multiple instances, or concurrent execution. There are various ways of making React components server-proof, hydration-proof, instance-proof, and composition-proof.

articles.title: INTRO TO PYTORCH
articles.summary: PyTorch is a popular deep learning framework by Meta AI. This visual learning guide begins by explaining tensors as its fundamental data type for numerically representing diverse data like text and images, detailing their initialization and mathematical operations. It then goes into Autograd, PyTorch's engine for automatic differentiation, which efficiently calculates gradients essential for neural network optimization via algorithms like gradient descent.

articles.title: HOW NOT TO ANSWER THE SALARY QUESTION
articles.summary: Job seekers should avoid answering the salary question too early in the interview process. Discussing salary before understanding the full job responsibilities and benefits is unhelpful for both parties and can lead to being undervalued. Candidates should deflect the question by focusing on industry-standard ranges, the comprehensive benefits package, or the detailed nature of the role itself.

articles.title: COMPONENTS WILL KILL PAGES
articles.summary: AI chat is replacing the need to click through websites, so instead of building page flows, companies should build component libraries that AI can understand and render inline. For example, Cloudflare can show you an interactive workflow diagram right inside ChatGPT.

articles.title: CLERK'S NEW PRICING: 50,000 MRUS FREE
articles.summary: First major pricing update since 2023. Free tier now covers 50,000 monthly retained users (up from 10,000 MAUs), MFA included in Pro at $20/mo, and unlimited apps on every plan. Automatic volume discounts as you scale.

articles.title: MODERN CSS
articles.summary: Modern CSS is a resource dedicated to updating developers on modern CSS practices, urging them to abandon outdated hacks from 2015. It shows modern, native CSS solutions side-by-side with the older, often JavaScript-reliant techniques they replace. The site covers a wide range of common challenges, including layout, form validation, animations, color management, and workflow improvements.

articles.title: WEBMCP
articles.summary: The WebMCP API is a new JavaScript interface that enables web apps to expose their functionality as "tools" for AI agents. Web developers can register these tools, which are JavaScript functions with natural language descriptions and structured schemas, through a ModelContext interface. This allows various agents, including LLM-based AI platforms, browser agents, and assistive technologies, to invoke client-side web application logic.

articles.title: TWO NEW SHOWBOAT TOOLS: CHARTROOM AND DATASETTE-SHOWBOAT
articles.summary: Chartroom and datasette-showboat expand the capabilities of a CLI tool, Showboat, which assists coding agents in creating Markdown documents to demonstrate their code. datasette-showboat enables Showboat's new remote publishing feature, allowing incremental updates of documents to a Datasette instance for real-time viewing, addressing the previous delay in seeing agent-generated content. Chartroom is a CLI charting tool designed for coding agents to easily generate and embed various chart types, such as bar, line, and scatter graphs, with alt text directly into Showboat documents.

articles.title: OWNING A $5M DATA CENTER
articles.summary: Comma.ai built its own $5M data center instead of using the cloud and estimates it saved $20M+ by doing it. Its 600 GPUs and 4PB of storage are cooled by big fans and San Diego air, all maintained by just a couple of engineers. The philosophy is that owning your compute forces better engineering.

articles.title: SAM “CLAWS” ATTENTION BACK OPENAI
articles.summary: OpenAI has hired Peter Steinberger, the developer behind the viral AI agent OpenClaw. This acquisition aims to revitalize OpenAI's coding tools and reclaim developer mindshare from rivals like Anthropic by focusing on practical, "embedded intelligence" agents. The hiring also provides OpenAI with a new narrative for investors about personal autonomous agents, while simultaneously being a win over competitors Meta and Anthropic.

articles.title: HARD PROBLEMS IN SOCIAL MEDIA ARCHIVING
articles.summary: Institutions face challenges in archiving social media, which differ greatly from individual efforts. Some difficulties include the overwhelming scale of content, the struggle to preserve private or ephemeral media, and capturing the dynamic user experience and opaque algorithms.

articles.title: WHAT YOUR BLUETOOTH DEVICES REVEAL ABOUT YOU
articles.summary: Widely enabled Bluetooth devices continuously broadcast signals that passively expose a surprising amount of personal metadata and presence patterns.

articles.title: USE PROTOCOLS, NOT SERVICES
articles.summary: Using decentralized communication protocols instead of centralized services is essential to protect user privacy.

articles.title: WHAT TO DO IF YOU TAKE AGI SERIOUSLY
articles.summary: AI's progress, fastest where correctness is cheaply verifiable, demands strategic adaptation to navigate economic shifts, mitigate profound risks, and leverage its potential.

articles.title: USING AI TO SHIFT E2E TEST MAINTENANCE LEFT
articles.summary: Monday.com engineers developed an AI system that analyzes E2E test failures during code review to provide contextual explanations tied to pull request changes.
